terms.definition: The first layer of a feedforward network that presents one value per input feature to the rest of the model; its width equals the input dimensionality.
Any intermediate layer between the input and output layers; each hidden neuron transforms the previous layer's activations with learned weights and a nonlinearity.
The final layer whose neurons produce the network's predictions; its width matches the number of outputs (e.g. class logits or a single regression value).
The basic compute unit in a neural network layer: it forms a weighted sum of its inputs plus a bias and passes the result through an activation function to produce one activation value.
The number of neurons in a given layer; wider layers can represent more distinct intermediate features in parallel at that stage.
In a feedforward MLP, the count of hidden layers stacked between input and output; deeper networks apply more sequential transformations to the data.
The numeric output of a neuron after its activation function; the vector of activations from one layer becomes the input vector to the next.
A layer where every neuron receives input from every neuron in the previous layer, stored as a dense weight matrix between the two activation vectors.
